events.title: Zine Club
events.description:
Join us every two weeks for Zine Club, a gathering dedicated to DIY bookmaking, self-publishing, creative experimentation, and community. Whether you’re new to zines or working on an ongoing project, this is a space to explore ideas, share work, and be creative in community with other artists, writers, activists, and creatives of all kinds.
Each begins with a quick demo of a technique or process you might incorporate in your own work. Explore a wide range of materials and techniques available in our studio, including collage, drawing, image transfer, stenciling, and printmaking.
Zine Club is led by Lista Studios instructor Wil Bigio, a graphic designer, illustrator, and dedicated doodler based in New York.
Details:
Meets every other Wednesday (first and third of the month) from 6:00 pm – 8:30 pm
Basic materials provided (paper, collage supplies, and printmaking tools)

events.title: Brotaku: Mario Kart Tournament Comedy Show
events.description:
Brotaku: Mario Kart Tournament Comedy Show
Stand-up comedians roast players while they compete live in a chaotic Mario Kart tournament.
16 competitors enter. One leaves with $250. Everyone else gets cooked.
Expect:
• stand-up to open the show
• player intros + live roasting
• wildcard players pulled from the audience
• unhinged energy the entire time
Part tournament, part comedy show, part public execution.
Like playing video games on your friend’s couch—if your friends were ruthless comedians.

Details:
• Thursday, May 21
• Doors 7:30 PM | Show 8:00 PM
• Eastville Comedy Club (Brooklyn)
• 2 drink minimum

Tickets:
• Competitor Pass ($27): guaranteed tournament spot
• Audience ($19): watch + chance to be pulled as a wildcard
Competitor slots fill fast. Last show sold out.
All tickets include 2 takoyaki balls + 1 meat skewer from Karl’s Balls and Izakaya Juraku.
events.vendor: Brotaku: Mario Kart Tournament Comedy Show

events.title: Guided Brewery Tour and Sake Tasting
events.description:
Join us for a tour of our state-of-the-art sake brewery in Industry City, Brooklyn. See first hand how we turn rice and water into delicious Brooklyn-made premium craft sake. Led by our expert brewery staff, this experience will walk you through our production facility and allow you to take in the sights, sounds and delicious smells of New York’s premier sake brewery. The tour concludes in our taproom where we will lead you a guided tasting flight of 3 kinds of Brooklyn Kura and introduce you to the nuance and variety of our award-winning sake.
Location:
Please arrive promptly in our taproom to start your tour. The location is:
34 34th Street, Inner courtyard
Industry City Building 6
Brooklyn, NY 11232
events.vendor: Guided Brewery Tour and Sake Tasting

events.title: Hifi Provisions Presents Black Lotus Band with Alvin Dan
events.description:
Black Lotus Band
NYC-based alternative RnB band working to develop their own sound and music that will capture listeners to be real and authentic to themselves — flaws and all.
Alvin Dan
At the core of Alvin Dan’s sound is emotional honesty. He describes himself simply as “a sad boi who plays indie R&B, jazz, and folk and writes love songs to cope,” but beneath that simplicity lies a deeper intention. His songwriting explores heartbreak and longing while challenging the extractive nature of the music industry. Rather than chasing individual success at the expense of creative autonomy, Alvin believes in building collectives—spaces where artists uplift one another and ensure communities are cared for. For him, music isn’t just expression; it’s infrastructure for connection and shared struggle.
All tickets will have a $1 fee that goes directly towards Hifi Provisions. All seats are first-come, first-served. Tickets can be purchased in person or online. A valid ID will need to be presented if drinking alcoholic beverages.
events.vendor: Hifi Provisions Presents Black Lotus Band with Alvin Dan

events.title: Rib King NYC 2026
events.description:
The hottest summer kickoff event is back! Rib King NYC brings you the best BBQ restaurants, chefs, and drink makers—all in one place.
We’re kicking off the summer of 2026 with our biggest Rib King NYC yet! Join us for Rib King NYC on May 23, 2026, at Industry City’s Courtyard 1/2, 635 2nd Avenue in Brooklyn.
Rib King NYC is presented by Food Karma Projects and NYC BBQ. The all-inclusive fest features 20+ chefs, restaurants, and BBQ experts smoking, saucing, grilling, and glazing their very best ribs, all vying for the 2026 Rib King crown. And we don’t stop at just the ribs. At Rib King NYC, you’ll also find even more standout bites, award-winning hot sauces, desserts, plus spirits, wine, hard cider, craft beer, and more.
Rib King NYC features ribs from some of New York’s most creative chefs and restaurants. As we finalize the roster for 2026, take a peek at 2025’s Rib King NYC chefs and restaurants for an idea of what to expect:
Vincent Mangual of Empire BBQ – 2025 Rib King NYC Champion
Anthony Scerri of Smoke Sweats with Achilles Heat – 2025 Rib King NYC Runner-Up, 2025 Brisket King® NYC Champion
Gavin, Jamie, and Chris Farella of Brothers BBQ – 2025 Rib King NYC Friends of Firefighters People’s Choice Award
Oak Marprasert of KAM RAI THAI – 2025 Pig Island NYC Best in Show
Darlene Lawrence of Sands Jerk Hut
Dinosaur Bar-B-Que
Cenobio Canalizo of Morgan’s Brooklyn BBQ
Fefe Anggono of Taste of Surabaya
Jose Perez of Archie Moore’s and The Meat Truck
Samuel Alleyne of Fire + Smoke BBQ and Big Bob Gibson’s Memphis in May Team
Mike’s Hot Honey Team
Francis Maling of Bad For Business Popups
and more
Stay tuned for the full lineup announcement coming soon. For the latest information and sneak peeks, visit the Rib King NYC website and follow along on social media @ribkingnyc.

events.title: Personalized Japanese Chopstick Engraving Event @ IPPIN Project
events.description:
Celebrate meaningful moments with a one-of-a-kind gift!
IPPIN Project, in collaboration with Style of Japan—renowned Wakasa chopstick artisans from Fukui—offers an extended, limited-time engraving service on a wider selection of traditional Japanese chopsticks.
Customize your chopsticks with a name or special message, with assistance from native Japanese staff for authentic engraving in Japanese characters.
Perfect for commemorations, special occasions, and Father’s Day gifts.
Visit IPPIN Project in-store or place your order online before the end of May to take advantage of this exclusive service.
Why it’s special:
In Japanese culture, each person traditionally has their own set of chopsticks, symbolizing individuality and personal connection. Because they are not shared, chopsticks are often designed in distinct colors and patterns—and personalized sets have long been cherished as thoughtful gifts.
Order Period: through May 31 (in-store & online)
Available for pickup or delivery from Mid-June
Store Location: Maker’s Guild at Building 5
Email: contract@ippinproject.com
events.vendor: Personalized Japanese Chopstick Engraving Event @ IPPIN Project

events.title: Industry City Gourmet Food and Drinks Tour
events.description:
Set against the iconic backdrop of Sunset Park’s historic waterfront, the massive, revitalized structures of Industry City form a breathtaking stage for an exclusive culinary journey. This premium food and cocktail tour is a curated exploration of taste and craft, transforming an evening into a memorable, high-end experience.
Your adventure begins with artistic bonbons at Lilac Chocolates, explores gourmet imports at Sahadi’s, and includes a refined whiskey tasting at Fort Hamilton Distillery, followed by a sophisticated sake flight. Savor additional artisanal discoveries within the awe-inspiring industrial halls.
Perfect as a distinctive date night, this tour is also fully customizable for private groups and corporate retreats. We specialize in tailoring this engaging experience for team-building and client entertainment, making it an ideal one-day corporate outing. A sophisticated mini-vacation for locals and a profound discovery for visitors.
events.vendor: Industry City Gourmet Food and Drinks Tour

events.title: Sake and Cheese Pairing Class and Brewery Tour
events.description:
Step away from that bottle of vino and join us to sample the absolute best beverage to pair with cheese – We are talking about sake! If you haven’t yet tried pairing craft sake and premium cheese, you are in for a revelation. We’ll explore a wide range of flavors and textures… and together, we’ll discover the most exciting combinations of sake styles and cheeses from around the world. The experience is fun, interactive and absolutely delicious! Join us at Brooklyn Kura to learn about sake where sake is made!
What’s included:
A welcome sake to get you started!
A brief intro to sake and cheese basics.
Guided Tasting of 5 Japanese craft sakes from different regions paired to perfection with 5 outstanding cheese varieties.
Exploration of sake and cheese production styles and terrior.
Enjoy some nibbles and spreads on your cheese board that will take the pairing over the top.
Handouts so you can bring your newfound sake and cheese knowledge home
Finish with a Guided tour through our new Sake Brewery
events.vendor: Sake and Cheese Pairing Class and Brewery Tour

events.title: Bookbinding Workshop with Pashariko Bookbinding
events.description:
Bookbinding workshop with with Pashariko Bookbinding using all secondhand papers and materials.
Learn some basic bookbinding with all secondhand papers and materials! In this evening workshop, we’ll be focusing in on the pamphlet. The pamphlet can become a little notebook for your pocket, a fun way to level up a zine, a perfect format for an artist book, a sweet gift, and so much more! We’ll talk about paper, different variations on the pamphlet stitch, and fun ways you can personalize and decorate your books. Spending an evening with your book has never been more hands on 🙂
This workshop is hosted at Brooklyn Creative Reuse. 254 36th Street, 2nd Floor, Space C244. May 27th, 7-10pm.
